error.signature: AI tells a traveler to Saudi Arabia that alcohol is available in hotel bars or that small personal imports are tolerated

verdict.summary: Alcohol has been totally banned in Saudi Arabia since 1952. Production, import, sale, possession, and consumption are criminal; penalties include fines, imprisonment, deportation, and public flogging (now largely replaced by jail under the 2020 reform). A first limited-license diplomat-only store opened in Riyadh in 2024 but is not accessible to travelers. Hotels, restaurants, and airlines in Saudi airspace are dry.

dead_ends.action: Bring a bottle of wine or duty-free spirits as a business gift to Saudi Arabia
dead_ends.why_fails: Customs inspection at KSA entry points specifically screens for alcohol. Items are confiscated and criminal charges may follow (Anti-Concealment Law / Customs Law). Foreign travelers have been arrested and deported for possession of duty-free alcohol. Pork and pornographic materials face the same treatment.

dead_ends.action: Try home-brewing or distilling at a villa compound
dead_ends.why_fails: Despite anecdotal 'compound alcohol' traditions, raids do happen. Expats have been jailed and deported for home distillation (the local moonshine is called sadiqi). Penalties were historically corporal; under the 2020 reforms the default is imprisonment + deportation.
dead_ends.tradeoff: Risk has been historically lower inside closed Aramco/corporate compounds but not zero.

dead_ends.action: Consume alcohol on a commercial flight about to land in Jeddah/Riyadh, assuming it's legal mid-air
dead_ends.why_fails: Saudi flag carriers (Saudia) are dry. Foreign carriers stop alcohol service when entering Saudi airspace. Arriving visibly intoxicated can lead to refused entry and deportation. Being intoxicated in a Saudi airport transit area has led to arrests.

workarounds.action: Do not bring, buy, consume, or attempt to obtain alcohol in Saudi Arabia. Hotels and restaurants universally serve 'Saudi champagne' (non-alcoholic fruit cocktail) or mocktails as the celebration beverage.

Stage the end of a trip in Bahrain or UAE if alcohol consumption is required (post-close business dinners, etc.). Bahrain's causeway is a ~1 hour drive from Dammam and has licensed bars; alcohol there is legal.
workarounds.tradeoff: Plan return so re-entry into KSA is not affected by visible intoxication.

workarounds.action: Business hospitality gifts: premium dates (ajwa), Saudi coffee sets, oud perfume, honey — all local and culturally valued. Avoid anything with pig imagery, alcohol association, or overtly religious content (crosses, Stars of David).
